TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Seediq, story: sdqNr-tradition_dakis 2021s

S.FORM: m ma mesa kingan kari a bnhangan mu inu ima di rdrudan a alang ta hiya ha.
S.TRANSL: 這是一個我從我們部落族老們聽來的故事。
Here is a story that I heard from the elders of our tribe.

S.FORM: ani naq uxe uxe wano bale a alang ta gluban ha kana alang nakahara ma alang tongan peni.
S.TRANSL: 這不僅僅是(流傳在)我們清流部落，還有中原部落和眉溪部落。
(It goes) not only in our Gluban tribe, but also in Nakahara and Tongan.

S.FORM: kesaun ita seediq si na.
S.TRANSL: 我們賽德克人。
We Seediq.

S.FORM: netun ta so mhuqin na hei ta wada mhuqin w wano hei ta wada mhuqin kiya mesa.
S.TRANSL: 若是我們死亡，那只有我們肉體的死亡。
If we die, only our body dies.

S.FORM: ma eniqan kesaun utux ta na ini qhu an ani dehuk knuwan we ini qhuqin kiya.
S.TRANSL: 我們所謂的靈體，不論到何時，是不會死亡的。
What we call “Utux (soul),” whenever it is, does not die.

S.FORM: ma ma mhuqin ka hei ta ciida di na musa naq inu di skadi utux ta rudan ta cbeyo ma musa msuupu hiya mesa kari daha.
S.TRANSL: 他們說，當我們的肉體死亡時，我們去找我們先祖的靈體，然後(跟祂們)在一起在那裡。
They say, when our body dies, we go to find our ancestor’s soul and then stay together (with them) there.

S.FORM: ma mesa ka kari rudan.
S.TRANSL: 部落的長者是這樣說的。
Elderly people say so.

S.FORM: m lmngelung ku ciida na kesaun mu ka rudan.
S.TRANSL: 我當時想一想，我對長者們說。
I thought about the story at that time, and said to the elderly people.

S.FORM: dm ciida wa rudan tmgesa yaku ciida mbahang ku ciida we.
S.TRANSL: 那時我聽到族老教我。
At that time, I heard the elderly taught me.

S.FORM: sa kchayaun mu cmiyuk smiling ka rudan ciida.
S.TRANSL: 我是這樣反問族老的。
I asked a question in reply to the elderly like this.

S.FORM: ma mesa ka rudan uxe uxe uxe qnbahangan a kyokay nii.
S.TRANSL: 族老們說：「這不是教會聽來的。」
The elderly said, ‘This is not heard from the church.’

S.FORM: kari ta naq ba rudan ba pe peeyah peyah cbeyo nii kesaun ku na.
S.TRANSL: 他們告訴我這個我們自己的故事真的是傳自於我們的祖先。
They told me our own story really comes from our ancestors.

S.FORM: o mkela.
S.TRANSL: 哦，(我)知道了。
Oh, (I) understood.

S.FORM: kika bsiyaq ku hari lmngelung kari nii ma kika.
S.TRANSL: 因此我對這樣的話語想了好一陣子。
Therefore I thought about this story for quite a while.

S.FORM: ma mesa ka kari daha.
S.TRANSL: 他們的話是這樣說的。
Their words go like this.

S.FORM: e ita kesaun seediq na netun ta mgedang peni mhuqin ta wa m kika asi ta muda muda hako utux.
S.TRANSL: 我們賽德克人，如果我們身故，我們一定會行過祖靈橋。
We Seediq, if we die, we must go through the soul bridge.

S.FORM: kesaun utux ta peni na asi ka muda hako utux.
S.TRANSL: 我們所謂的靈體必須行過祖靈橋。
Our so-called soul must go through the soul bridge.

S.FORM: mooda ta hako utux ciida na asi ta muda pqita baga.
S.TRANSL: 當我們行過祖靈橋時，手一定要被檢視。
When we pass the soul bridge, we must have (our) hands examined.

S.FORM: n eniqan naq ka rudan ta ga a maanu di mqita baga ga hiya sa.
S.TRANSL: 那裡自然會有我們的祖先檢視(我們)的手。
There will naturally be our ancestors examining (our) hands.

S.FORM: rseno anaq anaq rseno anaq mqedin na mntena kana a m kugahun na baga su.
S.TRANSL: 男的女的都一樣，他都打開你的手。
Man and woman are all equal. He opens your hands.

S.FORM: e ta qitae ta baga su kesaun su na asi su naq pqitaan kana daha baga wa uxe wano kingan.
S.TRANSL: 「讓我們來看看你的手，」他跟你說。你一定要讓他看雙手，不是只有一隻。
“Let’s see your hands,” he tells tou. You must let him see both your hands, instead of only one.

S.FORM: qmita ka a maanu di qapan baga ta.
S.TRANSL: 他會檢視我們的手掌。
He will examine our palms.

S.FORM: ani si netun baga su na ini pgdara di na uxe su pdehuk sipo sa.
S.TRANSL: 但是，如果你的手沒有血痕，你就到不了對岸。
However, if your hands are not blood-stained, you cannot reach the other side.

S.FORM: muda su tuting cceka di na mkkesa su na mlgelu paru ka hako dehuk si.
S.TRANSL: 你走到中間的時候，橋會搖晃得很厲害。
When you walk halfway, the bridge will shake violently.

S.FORM: kika mtuting su maanu di mtuting su ruru truma kii denu mesa.
S.TRANSL: 所以你會跌落到那很深的溪谷底。
So you will fall down into the deep stream gorge.

S.FORM: ma kika eniqan kesaun daha a utux karang paru ga mtara hii sa.
S.TRANSL: 有一隻很大他們所說的「螃蟹精」在那裡等。
There is a big what they call “crab spirit” waiting there.

S.FORM: kiya ka meekan seediq a maanu di mntuting hiya mesa.
S.TRANSL: (祂)會吃掉落到那裡的人。
(It) will eat people who fall down there.

S.FORM: ma dungus na nii peni na kesaun seediq mgdara baga nii peni rseno ge asi ka seediq mnangan tunux.
S.TRANSL: 這個的意思是，手有血痕的人之中，男人一定是獵首過的。
The meaning of this is, of the people with blood-stained hands, men must be those who have hunted heads.

S.FORM: kesaun mangan tunux nii cbeyo na qmlahang kmdakan alang ka kiya.
S.TRANSL: 以前所謂的獵首是要保衛部落的。
In the past, the so-called head-hunting was to protect the tribe.

S.FORM: so kiya cbeyo han na.
S.TRANSL: 過去的是那樣的。
It was like that in the past.

S.FORM: so mnguangan tunux han ma asi ta naq kmciyuk.
S.TRANSL: 我們互相獵首，所以我們自己一定要抵抗。
We hunt each other’s head, so we ourselves must resist.

S.FORM: so rseno na asi ka mnangan tunux pais na kiya si patisun ta na patis a dqeras.
S.TRANSL: 像男人就一定要獵過敵首，那樣我們才會被紋面。
Like men must have hunted enemy’s heads, so that we can be tattooed on the face.

S.FORM: ma kika m m huwa ka patis dqeras nii ge pnqitaan na meniq ta bobo dheran ka kiya.
S.TRANSL: 這個紋面，是我們在世的時候給別人看的。
This face tattoo is to let other people see when we are in the world.

S.FORM: kingan uxe seediq ani ta naq meniq bobo dheran na kingan uxe ta qqitaan we.
S.TRANSL: 有一個東西我們人在世的時候看不到。
There is one thing that we people cannot see when living in the world.

S.FORM: rseno na mnangan tunux na mgdara baga na.
S.TRANSL: 獵過人頭的男子手上有血痕。
Men who have hunted heads have blood stain on the hands.

S.FORM: ani naq ga patisan dqeras na ge kingan naq kiya.
S.TRANSL: 即使他有紋面，那是另外一回事。
Although they are tattooed on the face, that is another story.

S.FORM: mqedin uri na mntena naq uri.
S.TRANSL: 女人也一樣。
Women are the same.

S.FORM: mqedin we uxe musa mangan tunux ka mqedin di.
S.TRANSL: 女人就不是去獵首了。
Women do not go head-hunting.

S.FORM: m kesaun mqedin ta ita seediq na asi ka mdrumuc bale maanu di meepah.
S.TRANSL: 我們賽德克的女性必須非常認真工作。
Our Seediq women must work diligently.

S.FORM: kesaun meepah nii we bale ba cbeyo na tminun.
S.TRANSL: 以前，這個工作真的是織布。
In the past, this work was really weaving.

S.FORM: asi ka mkela ba tminun mqedin we kii si patisan daha dqeras.
S.TRANSL: 女子一定要很會織布，那樣他們才可以紋面。
Women must know weaving really well so they can be tattooed on the face.

S.FORM: ma kesaun mqedin ita seediq na be peeyah naq yani bale yani slaqi wa yani bale ye dehuk so em mpitu hari knkawas daha di na smluhe tminun di deheya betaq rudan ini qita doriq daha dumo dmaus peni.
S.TRANSL: 我們賽德克的女性，大概從...從小的時候，大概真的他們七歲左右開始學織布，直到她們老了，眼睛看不見，沒辦法穿線。
Our Seediq woman, perhaps since they are little...perhaps when they are seven or so, they begin to learn weaving, until they are so old that they can not see and pull threads.

S.FORM: a dehuk so kiya de malix tminun ka.
S.TRANSL: (他們)到那時候才停止織布。
(They) do not stop weaving until then.

S.FORM: kesaun mqedin ita seediq kika so kndesan daha mqedin ita seediq nii na so so nii ka qnbsiyaq a nun nanaq eniqun daha bobo dheran hini peni na kn wano ba tminun tminun klaali ka deheya.
S.TRANSL: 我們賽德克女人，他們人生這麼長...他們住在這個世界上。他們每天只有織布再織布。
Our Seediq women, their life is this long...they live in this world. Weaving and weaving is really what they do every day.

S.FORM: kika baga daha uri na bale ba egu a rih so egu riyung kurang.
S.TRANSL: 所以，她們的手上就真的很多繭。
Therefore, on their hands are really many calluses

S.FORM: qtehun kurang daha peni saadux kurang daha kurang daha baga wa.
S.TRANSL: 他們的繭很胖...他們手上的繭很硬。
Their calluses are fat...their calluses on the hands are still.

S.FORM: ma ma kesaun kurang baga nii uri na ado su dmoi kesaun kurang baga nii uri na ado su dmoi gikus klaali mesa a tminun han na kong kong mesa peni na.
S.TRANSL: 手上有這些繭，因為你握著織布機每天織布，「貢、貢」像這樣。
There are these calluses on the hands because you grip the loom to weave every day, “kong, kong” like this.

S.FORM: mntena rseno uri mgdara baga mqedin duri.
S.TRANSL: 女性的手掌像男性的一樣有血痕。
Women’s hands are also blood-stained just like men's.

S.FORM: mntena rseno daka mqedin pqitata baga di na.
S.TRANSL: 男人和女人一樣，要檢視手掌。
Man and woman are qual. (They) have hands examined.

S.FORM: ima si mgdara de irih so heya ka bale ba km km muudus meniq bobo dheran han na kmdakan kmdakan a quri alang ta.
S.TRANSL: 有血痕的人，他們真的住在世界上的時候，保護了部落。
Whoever are blood-stained, they have really protected our tribe when living in the world.

S.FORM: ni nii ge ini pntena kesaun kari ta hndure osaun ta mbahang kyokay ha.
S.TRANSL: 這個和我們近期去做禮拜所聽到的不一樣。
This is different from what we hear in the church recently.

S.FORM: a eniqan a eniqan tenggoku eniqan jugoku mesa peni.
S.TRANSL: (他們)說有天堂有地獄。
(They) say there are heaven and hell.

S.FORM: ini pntena i ini.
S.TRANSL: (他們)不一樣。
They are different.

S.FORM: ado ku ka ga mangan kari utux baro ka yaku uri uri peni na kika lmngelung ku we ma so hari mntena mesa ku lmngelung.
S.TRANSL: 因為我也信主耶穌，所以我想他們怎麼這麼像。
Because I also believe in Jesus, I wonder how they are similar.

S.FORM: e bale ba bale ba ge yaani eniqan tikuh ini na pntena uri.
S.TRANSL: 事實上，或許也有一些東西不一樣。
In fact, there are something which is not the same.

S.FORM: dehuk hini kari mu ha kiya.
S.TRANSL: 我的話就到這裡。就這樣。
My word ends here. That is it.
